parts.answer: Advantages Disadvantages Option 2 Choco • The market leader and sales are at their highest • Likely to be profitable since is a well-established brand and market leader. Profit is likely to support the development of new products • Has the highest market share and been a very successful brand for several years • Although competition may be high there are no new competitors entering this market • High promotional expenditure not required since it is a well-established brand • Could introduce extension strategies to revitalise sales • Is at the maturity/saturation stage of the product life cycle – so sales are likely to decline in the near future without extension strategies • May have to lower prices to retain high sales • Next move is to the decline stage and sales fall off quickly – falling prices and profits • Competitive pricing may lead to lower total revenue – lower profits Recommendation • It is better to stop producing Tiabar and keep Choco because this is the market leader, and it has many brand loyal customers. The sales are higher than Tiabar and it brings in more revenue and profit for CC. If sales start to fall slightly then extension strategies can be introduced to keep the sales high. • It is better to stop producing Choco and keep Tiabar as it is a new brand for CC and has steadily growing sales. This is a new market which has potentially higher sales in the future. Whereas Choco, although successful, may have limited opportunity for growth in the future. October/November Question Answer Marks Award 1 mark for each diseconomy of scale (max 2). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the diseconomy of scale – one of which must be applied to this context. Diseconomies might include: • Poor communication – more layers of hierarchy for messages to be transmitted after the takeover of NM – more likely to be distorted • Lack of commitment from employees – larger businesses may make employees feel unimportant as there are so many employ

Recommendation • Samuel should become the Operations Manager at NM because he is experienced in the way CC wants the factory to be run and the working practices it uses. He is very experienced and has worked for CC for 20 years. Ritesh is not familiar with the working practices at CC, and this might lead to errors and therefore lower efficiency. • Ritesh should be kept as Operations Manager because he has far more knowledge and experience of NM and its brands. He understands the market for chocolates in country Z and also has established links with suppliers of ingredients. This would be missing if Samuel tried to run the operations department in NM, resulting in lower efficiency. October/November Question Answer Marks Award 1 mark for each effect (max 4). Award a maximum of 1 additional mark for each explanation. There are no application marks for this question. Relevant effects might include: • Possibly improved pay – trade union negotiations make wage demands for a pay rise more likely to be successful • Improved working conditions – as trade union can put forward a case to the employer if conditions are not good • Employee views are represented in grievances with managers – more likely to be listened to as strength in numbers • Provides advice to members – such as if they feel they have been dismissed unfairly / unfairly treated, employees will know if they have a case against the business • The communication between employers and employees is better – as there is a channel through which issues/points from employees can be raised • Provides services to members – such as insurance or social facilities at reduced rates • Improved job satisfaction – by encouraging training • Improved job security – especially if it is a closed shop • Influence government policy e.g. pressure to increase legal minimum wage For example: A trade union will give advice to employees (1) such as if they feel they have been unfairly treated then the trade union can represent them in meeti

Conclusion • NM’s shareholders will find the information most useful because they need to assess how they might benefit from the takeover. CC’s managers will use other information about the efficiency of NM or its sales figures before making decisions about NM. The bank manager is only interested in the ability of the business to repay a loan. • CC’s managers will find the information most useful because they will be able to assess if CC is more efficient than NM and if so, they will need to make NM more efficient after the takeover. • CC’s bank manager will find the information most useful because they need to assess whether to offer a loan for the takeover and judge if the loan is likely to be repaid.
